task_description: Analyze transcript to define magical landing experience
reflection_q1: Could have been more concise in the execute phase — the inline analysis was thorough but long
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have used ContentAnalysis skill to extract transcript quotes more systematically rather than manual extraction
reflection_q3: ContentAnalysis skill was identified but not invoked — would have been useful for structured transcript extraction

task_description: Research 3D animation tools for reveal mode integration
reflection_q1: Should have challenged the 3D assumption earlier in OBSERVE rather than researching it fully
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have done a quick first-principles check on whether 3D is needed before launching 9 research agents — could have saved 3 agents on Three.js/R3F
reflection_q3: ContentAnalysis skill would have helped extract and verify MCP server capabilities from their actual repos

task_description: Synthesize 21st.dev Magic findings and update skill docs
reflection_q1: Should have waited for background researcher before making initial claims about 21st.dev capabilities
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have tested the builder tool first to establish baseline before running inspiration queries
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed - this was synthesis work

task_description: Build /reveal-cinematic route using cinematic-animation skill
reflection_q1: Could have used /simplify to catch any quality issues in the prototype code
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm might have spawned a browser agent to screenshot both routes for visual comparison
reflection_q3: playwright-skill could have been used to verify the route renders correctly

task_description: Evaluate geo-seo-claude repo for PAI integration
reflection_q1: WebFetch on raw GitHub URLs was efficient; should have also fetched uninstall.sh and brand_scanner.py
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have an automated external-skill-evaluation template with pre-built ISC criteria for compatibility assessment
reflection_q3: Could have used the Utilities CreateSkill canonicalization workflow to preview what the ported skill would look like

task_description: Critical quality review of GeoSeo skill files
reflection_q1: Should have given background agents explicit constraints on voice blocks and scoring weights to prevent inconsistencies
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would enforce a contract pattern - canonical weights in ScoringMethodology.md referenced not duplicated
reflection_q3: Should have run /simplify in the first Algorithm run - it was selected but never invoked which was a critical failure

task_description: Design and implement GeoSeo output directory with domain-keyed subdirectories
key_learnings: Filename consistency is the #1 integration risk in multi-workflow skills — Review caught 5 pre-existing mismatches between producing and consuming workflows
Single-path output beats two-stage promotion for LLM-executed workflows — fewer variables means fewer silent failures
Pointer pattern (see SKILL.md) works well for sub-workflows but orchestrator workflows appropriately inline instructions
Always grep for all variant names when modifying output files across a multi-file skill

task_description: GEO audit of rankless.ai website
reflection_q1: Should have parallelized all discovery fetches in one batch instead of two rounds
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have used the Research skill for comprehensive brand mention scanning beyond what the GeoAiVisibility agent could do alone
reflection_q3: Research skill for brand mention validation, potentially Council skill for synthesizing conflicting subagent signals

task_description: Run GeoSeo audit on airevolutionlabs.com website
reflection_q1: Should have parallelized all page fetches in a single batch rather than two sequential rounds
reflection_q2: Could have used the CitabilityScorer.py tool for more precise citability scoring instead of relying solely on subagent analysis
reflection_q3: Could have used the Research skill to find external brand mentions and competitive positioning data

task_description: In-depth analysis of CLI-Anything GitHub repo
reflection_q1: Should have proceeded directly with gh API rather than waiting for research agents to load context
reflection_q2: Clone repo locally for deeper static analysis; use structured parallel agent template
reflection_q3: Thinking skill (First Principles) for competitive analysis; Council for synthesizing multiple perspectives

task_description: Council debate CLI-Anything for real estate investors
reflection_q1: Should have used Task-based parallel agent spawning for the 4 council perspectives instead of serial generation. The debate would have produced more genuinely diverse perspectives through agent isolation.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have spawned 4 ComposeAgent instances per round for true parallel execution, and would have resolved the Skill tool naming convention before invocation attempts.
reflection_q3: Should have used the Agents skill to create custom agents for each council perspective, and Background Agents for parallel execution. The ComposeAgent pathway was the correct approach for custom council members.

task_description: Explore vibe-kanban repo for CLI-Anything compatibility
reflection_q1: Could have pre-screened for web-app-with-API mismatch faster
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have a 30-second pre-screen: Does this software already have a programmatic API?
reflection_q3: Thinking/FirstPrinciples could have sharpened the compatibility criteria into a reusable screener

task_description: Update cli-anything-explorer with software compatibility model
reflection_q1: Should have created compatibility model during vibe-kanban assessment proactively
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would auto-generate lessons-learned artifacts when evaluations reveal knowledge gaps
reflection_q3: Research skill could have validated scripting API claims for proprietary software

task_description: Brainstorm CLI-Anything pipelines for real estate investors
reflection_q1: Should have checked skill availability before selecting capabilities
reflection_q2: Parallelize both segments with background agents for speed
reflection_q3: WebSearch for CLI command validation, background agents for parallel segment work

task_description: Brainstorm CLI-Anything pipelines for real estate investors
reflection_q1: Should have checked skill availability before selecting capabilities
reflection_q2: Parallelize both segments with background agents for speed
reflection_q3: WebSearch for CLI command validation, background agents for parallel segment work

task_description: Brainstorm CLI-Anything pipelines for three RE segments
reflection_q1: Should have verified skill availability before selecting capabilities. Linear brainstorming may have missed better ideas vs diverge-converge.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have used parallel agents per segment, then cross-pollinated. Would also have done a diverge phase with 30+ raw ideas before filtering to 6.
reflection_q3: Research skill could have verified CLI access claims for each software tool. Background agents could have parallelized per-segment brainstorming.

task_description: Extensive analysis of Gastown GitHub repo
reflection_q1: Parallel background agents for docs fetching was efficient. Key insight (complementary layers) required deep reading before emerging.
reflection_q2: Should have fetched source code files (main.go, internal/) to verify technical claims alongside docs.
reflection_q3: Thinking/FirstPrinciples skill should have been invoked for synthesis section — sharper decomposition of what both systems are really solving.

task_description: Deploy NanoClaw on Ubuntu 24.04 with CUDA GPU services
reflection_q1: Should have pre-validated nvm vs system node paths and embedding dtype before starting services
reflection_q2: Parallelized all file edits upfront and kicked off both model downloads simultaneously from start
reflection_q3: Research skill was available but not needed - deployment guide was comprehensive enough

task_description: Gap analysis OAuth auto-refresh vs current implementation
reflection_q1: Could have confirmed token lifetime with a second data point instead of one observation
reflection_q2: Launched research agent but did not need to wait for it — CLI source had everything
reflection_q3: Research skill could have checked Anthropic public docs for OAuth refresh endpoint documentation

task_description: Gap analysis OAuth auto-refresh vs current implementation
reflection_q1: Could have confirmed token lifetime with a second data point instead of one observation
reflection_q2: Launched research agent but did not need to wait for it — CLI source had everything
reflection_q3: Research skill could have checked Anthropic public docs for OAuth refresh endpoint documentation

task_description: Design doc for transcription service logging framework
reflection_q1: Investigation was thorough but could have been more concise in the observe phase
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have recognized the three-layer visibility gap pattern immediately from the code structure
reflection_q3: Could have used FirstPrinciples skill to decompose the error visibility problem more systematically

task_description: Implement transcription logging per implementation spec
reflection_q1: Execution was clean — well-defined spec made implementation straightforward
reflection_q2: Could have tested with a real TikTok URL to verify success path logging too, not just failure
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed — the spec was precise enough for direct implementation
